The throttle is isenthalpic, so h₄ = h₃ = 248.85 kJ/kg.

Quality at state 4 (two-phase at -4°C): x₄ = (h₄ - h_f)/(-4°C))/(h_g - h_f at -4°C) = (248.85 - 194.71)/(395.93 - 194.71) = 0.269. Then s₄ = s_f + x₄(s_g - s_f) = 1.1819 kJ/(kg·K) at -4°C.

For the compressor (η_comp = 0.82), first determine the isentropic exit (s_{2s} = s₁ = 1.7293 kJ/(kg·K) at P_cond). This state is superheated (s_{2s} > s_g at 35°C). Saturation properties for R-134a are obtained from tables based on the specified IIR reference state by interpolating to T = -11°C for the evaporator and T = 48°C for the condenser.

State 1 (sat. vapor at -11°C): \(h_1 = h_g\), \(s_1 = s_g\).

State 3 (sat. liquid at 48°C): \(h_3 = h_f\), \(s_3 = s_f\).

State 4: \(h_4 = h_3\) (isenthalpic), two-phase at -11°C. Quality \(x_4 = (h_4 - h_f)/(-11^\circ\text{C})/h_{fg}(-11^\circ\text{C})\). Then \(s_4 = s_f(-11^\circ\text{C}) + x_4 \cdot s_{fg}(-11^\circ\text{C})\).

State 2s: \(P_2 = P_\text{sat}(48^\circ\text{C})\), \(s_{2s} = s_1\), superheated vapor (interpolated in superheated tables at nearest pressure). Then \(h_{2s}\) is found.

Actual compressor: \(\eta_\text{comp} = (h_{2s} - h_1)/(h_2 - h_1) = 0.83\), solved for \(h_2\).

Then \(s_2\) is found by interpolation in superheated tables at \(P_2\), \(h_2\).

Per-unit-mass energy quantities:
- \(w_\text{comp} = h_2 - h_1\)
- \(q_L = h_1 - h_4\)
- \(q_H = h_2 - h_3\)
- \(\text{COP}_R = q_L/w_\text{comp}\)

With \(\dot{m} = 0.21\) kg/s:
- \(\dot{W}_\text{comp} = \dot{m} \cdot w_\text{comp}\)
- \(\dot{Q}_L = \dot{m} \cdot q_L\) (capacity)

Entropy generation per unit mass for each component (from steady-flow entropy balance on each CV):
- Compressor (adiabatic): \(s_{\text{gen,comp}} = s_2 - s_1\)
- Condenser (heat rejected to \(T_\text{env} = 307.6\) K): \(s_{\text{gen,cond}} = (s_3 - s_2) + q_H/T_\text{env}\)
- Throttle (adiabatic): \(s_{\text{gen,throttle}} = s_4 - s_3\)
- Evaporator (heat absorbed from \(T_\text{space} = 268.5\) K): \(s_{\text{gen,evap}} = (s_1 - s_4) - q_L/T_\text{space}\)

Total: \(s_{\text{gen,total}} =\) sum of component values (verifiable as \(q_H/T_\text{env} - q_L/T_\text{space}\)).

Energy balance error is the deviation from \(q_L + w_\text{comp} - q_H = 0\) (due to rounding in table interpolation).

Saturation properties are obtained from R-134a tables using the specified IIR reference state. State 1 is saturated vapor at T_evap = -14°C, directly giving h₁, s₁ and P_evap. State 3 is saturated liquid at T_cond = 45°C, directly giving h₃, s₃ and P_cond. The throttle is isenthalpic, so h₄ = h₃. Quality x₄ is calculated from the definition x₄ = (h₄ - h_f)/h_fg at P_evap.

For the compressor, s_{2s} = s₁ at P_cond locates state 2s in the superheated table, giving h_{2s}. The given compressor efficiency is rearranged to give the actual enthalpy change: h₂ - h₁ = (h_{2s} - h₁)/η_comp, yielding h₂. State 2 is then located in the superheated table at P_cond and h₂ to obtain s₂. State 4 is in the two-phase region at P_evap; s₄ is calculated from s_f, s_g and x₄ at P_evap.

Per-unit-mass energy quantities follow directly: w_comp = h₂ - h₁, q_L = h₁ - h₄, q_H = h₂ - h₃. COP_R = q_L/w_comp. Multiplication by the given mass flow rate supplies the power and heat transfer rates. The dead-state properties h₀ and s₀ are obtained from the superheated table at T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Flow exergies are then ef_i = (h_i - h₀) - T₀(s_i - s₀).

Entropy generation per unit mass for each component accounts for the external reservoirs: T_H = 305.1 K for the condenser and T_L = 265.9 K for the evaporator. Thus s_gen,comp = s₂ - s₁, s_gen,cond = s₃ - s₂ + q_H/T_H, s_gen,throttle = s₄ - s₃, and s_gen,evap = s₁ - s₄ - q_L/T_L. The total is the sum of the component values. Exergy destructions follow from x_dest,i = T₀ × s_gen,i. The Carnot COP uses the reservoir temperatures: COP_Carnot = T_L/(T_H - T_L). The second-law efficiency is the ratio of actual to Carnot COP. Energy balance closure on the cycle is verified to machine precision (error = 0).

For the compressor, s_{2s} = s₁ at P_cond locates state 2s in the superheated table, giving h_{2s}. The given compressor efficiency is rearranged to give the actual enthalpy change: h₂ - h₁ = (h_{2s} - h₁)/η_comp, yielding h₂. State 2 is then located in the superheated table at P_cond and h₂ to obtain s₂. State 4 is in the two-phase region at P_evap; s₄ is calculated from s_f, s_g and x₄ at P_evap.

Per-unit-mass energy quantities follow directly: w_comp = h₂ - h₁, q_L = h₁ - h₄, q_H = h₂ - h₃. COP_R = q_L/w_comp. Multiplication by the given mass flow rate supplies the power and heat transfer rates. The dead-state properties h₀ and s₀ are obtained from the superheated table at T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Flow exergies are then ef_i = (h_i - h₀) - T₀(s_i - s₀).

Entropy generation per unit mass for each component accounts for the external reservoirs: T_H = 305.1 K for the condenser and T_L = 265.9 K for the evaporator. Thus s_gen,comp = s₂ - s₁, s_gen,cond = s₃ - s₂ + q_H/T_H, s_gen,throttle = s₄ - s₃, and s_gen,evap = s₁ - s₄ - q_L/T_L. The total is the sum of the component values. Exergy destructions follow from x_dest,i = T₀ × s_gen,i. The Carnot COP uses the reservoir temperatures: COP_Carnot = T_L/(T_H - T_L). The second-law efficiency is the ratio of actual to Carnot COP. Energy balance closure on the cycle is verified to machine precision (error = 0).

Use the IIR reference state for R-134a properties: h = 200.0 kJ/kg and s = 1.0000 kJ/(kg·K) for saturated liquid at 0°C.

Determine all state enthalpies, entropies, and flow exergies. Calculate compressor work, cooling effect, heat rejection, x₄, and COP_R. Find entropy generation and exergy destruction in each component (compressor, condenser, throttle, evaporator), total exergy destruction, Carnot COP, and second-law efficiency (η_II = COP_R / COP_Carnot).

Present your final answers in the following format:
h₁ = ___ kJ/kg
h₂s = ___ kJ/kg
h₂ = ___ kJ/kg
h₃ = ___ kJ/kg
h₄ = ___ kJ/kg
w_comp = ___ kJ/kg
q_L = ___ kJ/kg
q_H = ___ kJ/kg
x₄ = ___

s₃ = ___ kJ/(kg·K)

s_gen,comp = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,cond = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,throttle = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,evap = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen,total = ___ kJ/(kg·K)

x_dest,total = ___ kJ/kg
COP_Carnot = ___
η_II = ___

**Reasoning:** Saturation properties for R-134a are obtained from IIR-reference tables at T_evap = -22°C and T_cond = 41°C (with interpolation from neighboring temperatures -20°C/-24°C and 40°C for exact match to given temperatures). This yields P_evap = 121.46 kPa, P_cond = 1.044 MPa, state 1 (sat vapor): h₁, s₁; state 3 (sat liquid): h₃, s₃. State 4 is isenthalpic (h₄ = h₃), with quality x₄ computed from h₄ relative to hf/hg at T_evap. For the non-isentropic compressor (η_comp = 0.83), s_{2s} = s₁ at P_cond in the superheated region; h_{2s} is found by determining the superheat ΔT from Δs = c_p ln(T_{2s}/T_sat) (c_p ≈ 0.966 kJ/kg·K), giving h_{2s} from hg + c_pΔT at P_cond. Actual h₂ follows from the efficiency definition. q_L = h₁ - h₄, w_comp = h₂ - h₁, q_H = h₂ - h₃, COP_R = q_L/w_comp. Mass flow rates are ṁ × specific values. Entropies at state 2 (superheated at h₂, P_cond) and state 4 (two-phase) are found similarly using c_p ln(T/T_sat) for s₂ and s_f + x s_fg for s₄. Component entropy generations follow from the steady-flow balance s_gen = (s_out - s_in) - Σ(q_j/T_b), using T_b = 308.1 K for condenser heat rejection and T_b = 258.3 K for evaporator heat absorption (adiabatic compressor and throttle). Total s_gen is the sum. Dead-state properties (h₀ ≈ 419.66 kJ/kg, s₀ ≈ 1.8883 kJ/kg·K) at T₀ = 298.15 K, P₀ = 0.1 MPa (superheated vapor) are found by adjusting from the 0°C saturated vapor reference using c_p ≈ 0.852 kJ/kg·K and the ideal-gas entropy correction -R ln(P₀/P_sat). Flow exergies are ef_i = (h_i - h₀) - T₀(s_i - s₀). Exergy destructions are T₀ × s_gen for each component (total is the sum). COP_Carnot uses the given reservoir temperatures as T_L/(T_H - T_L). Second-law efficiency is COP_R/COP_Carnot. Cycle energy balance closes with error = 0.

**Reasoning**: Saturation properties are obtained from standard R-134a tables using the specified IIR reference state at states 1 (sat. vapor, -25°C) and 3 (sat. liquid, 40°C). State 4 is found from isenthalpic throttling (h₄ = h₃) and x₄ from the definition of quality at evaporator pressure. State 2s is found at condenser pressure and s₂s = s₁ by determining the superheat required to match entropy (using cp ≈ 0.96 kJ/kg·K for vapor to solve for ΔT from Δs and integrating for Δh). Actual compressor exit (state 2) follows from the given isentropic efficiency η_comp = (h₂s - h₁)/(h₂ - h₁). Per-mass energy transfers are q_L = h₁ - h₄, w_comp = h₂ - h₁, and q_H = h₂ - h₃. Mass flow rate gives the power and heat rate. COP_R = q_L/w_comp. Entropies at all states are from tables (s₄ from quality at evaporator pressure). Component entropy generations follow from steady-flow entropy balance on each CV: s_gen,comp = s₂ - s₁ (adiabatic), s_gen,throttle = s₄ - s₃ (adiabatic), s_gen,cond = (s₃ - s₂) + q_H/T_env (T_env = 302.5 K), and s_gen,evap = (s₁ - s₄) - q_L/T_L (T_L = 255.9 K). Total s_gen is the sum. Dead-state properties (h₀, s₀) at T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa are found by integrating cp(T) from saturated vapor at -25°C (with ideal-gas correction for small pressure difference). Flow exergies are ef_i = (h_i - h₀) - T₀(s_i - s₀) with T₀ = 298.15 K. Exergy destructions per unit mass are x_dest = T₀·s_gen for each component (total is the sum). COP_Carnot = T_L/(T_env - T_L). Second-law efficiency is η_II = COP_R/COP_Carnot. Energy balance closes exactly on the cycle (error = 0). All values are cross-checked for consistency with hfg = T·sfg at saturation states.
